summary:
I've identified the root cause of the issue: when a Raft follower receives and applies an `InstallSnapshot` RPC, it needs to ensure that its `commitIndex` is updated to at least the snapshot's `LastIncludedIndex`. Without this, a restart could incorrectly reset the node's `commitIndex` to `0`, resulting in a bug where duplicate log entries get re-applied to the state machine during recovery.

I have updated the `InstallSnapshot` function in `app/snapshot.go` to update the `commitIndex` alongside `lastApplied`:
```go
	// Update lastApplied — the state machine now reflects everything
	// up to and including the snapshot's last index.
	n.lastApplied = snap.LastIncludedIndex
	if snap.LastIncludedIndex > n.commitIndex {
		n.commitIndex = snap.LastIncludedIndex
	}
```

Running `./test.sh` now completes with success, confirming the fix prevents the duplicate entries bug:
```
=== Raft Snapshot Commit-Gap Test ===
Building...
--- Round 1 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 1: OK
--- Round 2 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 2: OK
--- Round 3 ---
PASS: All 13 keys present, no duplicate applies
Round 3: OK
PASS: No duplicate state machine transitions across all rounds
```
